description:
You are assisting an IT-support technician who maintains a simple CSV register of help-desk tickets.  
The register is already present on the system at:

/home/user/tickets/tickets.csv

1. File structure  
   • Comma-separated, UTF-8 encoded.  
   • The first line is a header.  
   • Column order (1-based):  
     1. TicketID 2. User 3. Issue 4. Status 5. Priority

2. Your goal  
Create a new text file named

/home/user/ticket_status.txt

that must contain ONLY the TicketID and Status of every ticket, in their original order, formatted exactly like this:

<ticket-id>:<status>

NO spaces before or after the colon and NO header row.  
A correct file should therefore look like the following five-line example (line endings = Unix “\n”):

TCK-0001:Open
TCK-0002:In Progress
TCK-0003:Resolved
TCK-0004:Open
TCK-0005:Resolved

(The concrete IDs and statuses will, of course, match whatever is inside the provided CSV.)

3. Constraints & hints  
• Use only standard command-line utilities already available on a minimal Linux install; a typical solution needs at most 2–3 commands (think of tools such as “cut” and “paste”).  
• Do NOT modify or delete the original CSV.  
• The new file /home/user/ticket_status.txt must be writable by the current non-root user (this will be the case automatically if you create it inside /home/user).  
• Running the command “wc -l /home/user/ticket_status.txt” must output the same number of data lines that are present in the CSV (the header row is not counted).  
• The automated test will:  
  1. Check that /home/user/ticket_status.txt exists.  
  2. Verify its content byte-for-byte (including line-break positions) against the expected format described above.  

4. Deliverable  
When you believe the task is complete, you may show a quick “cat” of /home/user/ticket_status.txt so the technician can visually confirm the result, but make sure the file itself is in place with the exact required content—this is what the automated judge will grade.
truth:
Initial state (present before the agent starts):

1. Directory: /home/user/tickets/
   Permission: 755 (user writable)

2. File: /home/user/tickets/tickets.csv  
   Permission: 644  
   Contents (Unix line endings):
   TicketID,User,Issue,Status,Priority
   TCK-1001,alice,Cannot login,Open,High
   TCK-1002,bob,Printer offline,In Progress,Medium
   TCK-1003,charlie,VPN issue,Resolved,Low
   TCK-1004,dana,Email bounce,Open,Medium
   TCK-1005,ed,Password reset,Resolved,Low

Expected post-task artefact:

File: /home/user/ticket_status.txt  
Permission: 644 (created by the agent, default umask assumed)  
Exact content (Unix line endings, 5 lines, no trailing blank line):

TCK-1001:Open
TCK-1002:In Progress
TCK-1003:Resolved
TCK-1004:Open
TCK-1005:Resolved
name: task_000000_cfe02312
